Boiling Brains is a product design partner for tech teams who value clarity, speed, and focus.

We specialize in designing functional digital products, web and mobile, with a calm, structured approach.

The logo is built on an inclined axis, which creates a sense of movement and rhythm. The composition is assembled from elements 
of different saturation and font size, but united by a common direction and vertical center. The upper and lower blocks visually support 
the main part, creating a balanced and dense structure. A wide grotesque is used, emphasizing 
the technical and applied nature of the team. The overall form maintains readability 
at different scales and is suitable for both 
digital and printed media.

children.name: vertical-spacing---all-vertical-spacing-is-defined-in-relation-to-the-height-of-the-capital-letter--b--from-the-main-logotype--the-spacing-between-the-ui-ux-tag-and-the-main-title-boiling-is-0-32b-the-spacing-between-brains-and-the-lower-tag-partners-is-0-4b
children.content:
Vertical Spacing:
All vertical spacing is defined in relation to the height of the capital letter "B" from the main logotype.

The spacing between the UI/UX tag and the main title Boiling is 0.32B

The spacing between Brains and the lower tag Partners is 0.4B

children.name: safe-zones---the-spacing-around-the-logo-is-equal-to-the-height-of-a-capital--b--on-all-four-sides-
children.content:
Safe Zones:
The spacing around the logo is equal to the height of a capital "B" on all four sides.

children.content: Main ColorsThis color palette reflects Boiling Brains’ approach to UI/UX design, practical, calm, and human. Each shade was selected not only for aesthetic balance but for its connection to the everyday tools and environments of digital product work.

children.name: make-digital-products-clearer--faster--and-stronger-without-adding-complexity-
children.content: Make digital products clearer, faster, and stronger without adding complexity.

children.name: how-the-market-perceives-us--how-we-define-ourselves-to-others-
children.content: How the market perceives us. How we define ourselves to others.

A thoughtful partner with calm authority.

Keeps things structured. Notices details. Doesn't raise their voice to be heard.
Not trying to lead the room, but when they speak, people listen.

Steady, focused, direct. Listens more than talks. Thinks before acting.
Reliable, respectful, and good at what they do, no show needed.

The logo is built on an inclined axis, which creates a sense of movement and rhythm. The composition is assembled from elements 
of different saturation and font size, but united by a common direction and vertical center. The upper and lower blocks visually support 
the main part, creating a balanced and dense structure. A wide grotesque is used, emphasizing 
the technical and applied nature of the team. The overall form maintains readability 
at different scales and is suitable for both 
digital and printed media.

The logotype is based on the Sequel 100 Wide typeface set at 85 pt, with custom adjustments.To distinguish it from standard headings and enhance visual softness, the letterforms were optimized: corners slightly rounded, edges smoothed, and overall contours refined. This gives the logo a more fluid and finished appearance, while retaining structural clarity.

The supporting elements, such as UI/UX, Partners (main), Design Partners, Product Partners, Design School, or Summer 2025 etc., use the same Sequel 100 Wide typeface, at lettering spacing 3%, at a reduced scale (approximately 60–70% of the primary size) and lowered opacity (70–80%) depending on context and background. These secondary labels provide contextual flexibility while maintaining visual unity with the core wordmark.

children.content:
Vertical Spacing:
All vertical spacing is defined in relation to the height of the capital letter "B" from the main logotype.

The spacing between the UI/UX tag and the main title Boiling is 0.32B

The spacing between Brains and the lower tag Partners is 0.4B

children.name: the-bb-monogram-is-a-simplified-version-of-the-main-logo----use-when----full-wordmark-doesn-t-fit---you-need-compact-visuals--avatars--favicons--merch--etc--
children.content:
The BB monogram is a simplified version of the main logo. Use when:
• Full wordmark doesn’t fit
• You need compact visuals (avatars, favicons, merch, etc.)

children.name: flat-mark-with-safe-zone-56-px
children.content: Flat mark with safe zone56 px

children.name: used-for-headlines--key-messages--and-bold-statements----this-typeface-feels-confident--geometric--and-slightly-unconventional----its-wide--low-contrast-characters-offer-excellent-readability-at-large-sizes--making-it-ideal-for-brand-presence-and-structure-
children.content:
Used for headlines, key messages, and bold statements.
This typeface feels confident, geometric, and slightly unconventional. Its wide, low-contrast characters offer excellent readability at large sizes, making it ideal for brand presence and structure.

children.name: used-for-body-text--captions--and-ui-adjacent-contexts----inter-is-neutral--modern--and-highly-legible---it-adapts-well-across-platforms-and-languages--providing-a-calm-and-reliable-foundation-for-longer-reading-and-dense-layouts-
children.content:
Used for body text, captions, and UI-adjacent contexts.
Inter is neutral, modern, and highly legible. 
It adapts well across platforms and languages, providing a calm and reliable foundation for longer reading and dense layouts.

children.name: main-colors--this-color-palette-reflects-boiling-brains--approach-to-ui-ux-design--practical--calm--and-human--each-shade-was-selected-not-only-for-aesthetic-balance-but-for-its-connection-to-the-everyday-tools-and-environments-of-digital-product-work-
children.content: Main ColorsThis color palette reflects Boiling Brains’ approach to UI/UX design, practical, calm, and human. Each shade was selected not only for aesthetic balance but for its connection to the everyday tools and environments of digital product work.

children.name: secondary-colors--these-muted-tones-are-lighter-and-more-airy--designed-to-support-the-main-identity-without-drawing-too-much-attention--they-provide-space--softness---and-a-sense-of-calm---like-the-white-margins-around-a-good-layout-
children.content: Secondary ColorsThese muted tones are lighter and more airy, designed to support the main identity without drawing too much attention. They provide space, softness, and a sense of calm — like the white margins around a good layout.

children.name: black-and-white--these-colors-are-used-when-needed-white-as-is-reference-of-the-default-web-background--black-is-softened-to-avoid-harshness-and-feel-more-balanced-
children.content:
Black and WhiteThese colors are used when needed
White as is reference of the default web background, Black is softened to avoid harshness and feel more balanced.

children.name: why-we-maintain-a-media-presence
children.content: Why We Maintain a Media Presence

Media is our way of speaking to the world. We don’t use platforms mostly for advertising, we use them to share our thinking, methods, and experiences. We build recognition through honesty, consistency, and ideas, not hype. Our presence in the media space helps attract conscious clients and form a professional community around our philosophy.

Thoughts on design, process, and handoff
Personal posts by Mart and Dima about work and life
Project case studies and reviews
Notes on working with developers
The routine and “unseen” parts of our work
Visual snippets, quotes, tools, and methods
Reflections on the profession and collaboration with others
Posts about teamwork and roles

children.name: we-use-bold-color-blocks--minimalist-typography--and-draw-inspiration-from-bauhaus-style--as-well-as-ui-layouts-and-technical-diagrams-of-design-tools-like-figma----everything-we-post-should-be-recognizable--readable--and-maintain-clarity-even-within-the-noise-of-social-media-feeds-
children.content: We use bold color blocks, minimalist typography, and draw inspiration from Bauhaus style, as well as UI layouts and technical diagrams of design tools like Figma. Everything we post should be recognizable, readable, and maintain clarity even within the noise of social media feeds.
